title: TANF - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > TANF 62 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON ONE ELIGIBILITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an online application portal for public assistance programs, including the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP/food stamps), Oregon Health Plan (OHP), Employment Related Daycare (ERDC), and Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF). Applicants submit one application to be considered for all programs for which they are eligible. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Eligibility requirements vary by program and depend on household size and household income. Refer to federal poverty level (FPL) chart for details. Hours: ONE Customer Service Center: Monday-Friday 7am-6pm Email (800) 699-9075 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF BAKER, UNION, AND WALLOWA COUNTIES BAKER CITY SELF-SUFFICIENCY OFFICE TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE FOR NEEDY FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides cash assistance to low-income families with children while they strive to become self-sufficient. Cash assistance is intended to meet a family's basic needs, such as food, clothing, shelter, and utilities. TANF recipients may be able to participate in the Jobs Opportunity and Basic Skills (JOBS) program, which assists with obtaining a high school diploma or GED, as well as job preparation and life skills. 3165 10th Street Suite 100 Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: -Low-income families with children under age 18 or pregnant women in the 9th month of pregnancy or if the parent is under age 18 must be head of their household -Live in Oregon -Have very low or no income and very few resources (such as property, vehicles, or bank accounts) The applicant must be a US Citizen or a qualified non-citizen. Parents without US Citizenship can apply for benefits for children who are US Citizens. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 523-3648 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES CAF BAKER, UNION, AND WALLOWA COUNTIES ENTERPRISE SELF-SUFFICIENCY AND CHILD WELFARE OFFICE TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE FOR NEEDY FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides cash assistance to low-income families with children while they strive to become self-sufficient. TANF recipients may be able to participate in the Jobs Opportunity and Basic Skills (JOBS) program, which assists with obtaining a high school diploma or GED, as well as job preparation and life skills. 635 SE 223rd Avenue Suite 102 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: This program is for families that: -Live in Oregon -Have little or no income and very few resources (like property, vehicles, or bank accounts) The person applying must: -Be the parent or caregiver for a child who is 18 or younger, or -Be pregnant, or -Be 18 or younger and head of their household -The applicant must be a US citizen or a qualified non-citizen. Parents without US citizenship can apply for benefits for children who are US citizens.
